response.reasoning: Candidate has 15+ years of software development experience, well beyond the 6+ year requirement. Current role at re:cinq involves full-stack TypeScript/Node.js work plus AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which directly aligns with the AI agent development requirement. Prior roles at Optilyz (TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB) and RIDE Capital (Node, TypeScript, GraphQL with TDD/CI/CD practices) match the primary stack well. Skills list confirms TypeScript, MongoDB, Docker, SQL, Java, Linux, Git. No explicit mention of Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin, or cloud platforms beyond AWS CDK at Optilyz. React experience is present but limited to Optilyz. Strong testing/TDD background (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos) aligns well with quality focus. Education claim of MIT BS 2009-2012 overlaps with full-time work at MGSoftware and Apon in the same period, which is a red flag worth noting but not disqualifying. Workshops/talks and OSS contributions demonstrate strong communication and craftsmanship.
strengths: Direct AI-agent/MCP infrastructure experience at re:cinq matches a key requirement
Strong full-stack TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB background from Optilyz and RIDE
Deep testing and TDD culture (Trial, Fluent-Asserts, Selenium course, workshops)
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed in resume
Kotlin not mentioned; Java experience is older (robotic arms, LabWindows era)
Overlapping MIT education dates with concurrent full-time roles raise a verification flag

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js / Vitest in stated stack
Strong testing and engineering craftsmanship track record

response.justification: The candidate substantially exceeds the experience bar and hits the most differentiating requirement — AI-agent development with MCP — while bringing the core TypeScript/Node/React/MongoDB stack from recent senior roles. Gaps around Nest.js, Vitest, and Kotlin are learnable for someone with this breadth and demonstrated polyglot history, though they should be probed in interview alongside the education timeline.
